competition.narrative: Competing head-to-head against Coupa and GEP Worldwide in a non-binding quote collection and upcoming RFP. Kamil named all three vendors 14 Apr ('looking at a Zycus, Coupa, GEP type solution') and is gathering budgetary estimates to validate feasibility before issuing the RFP. Price sensitivity is real: Kamil flagged 14 Apr that Waseef may be 'too small' for enterprise S2P, and the buyer is testing budget with non-binding quotes. Reaction to Zycus estimate 8 Jun was 'not a showstopper,' meaning competitive but not compellingly differentiated. GEP will likely price-compete aggressively on their cloud-native platform; Coupa may undercut on brand and SaaS simplicity. Zycus wedge: the 10-module suite (including Merlin AI intake, negotiation agent, risk) offers deeper AI and full S2P integration vs Coupa's lightweight procurement or GEP's consulting-heavy model. How we win: quantify ROI to justify the premium, lead with Merlin/AI differentiation in the RFP response, prove platform TCO advantage over 3–5 years, and convert Kamil to advocate internally for strategic depth vs low-cost feature parity.

deltas.subject: AI DIFFERENTIATION IS THE WEDGE: Buyer explicitly interested in AI (Merlin intake, agentic Anna, contract AI review) and Kamil expressed enthusiasm ('I liked it, actually').
